'''Trascor''' was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="AlienEncounters" /> located in the [[Trascor system/Legends|Trascor system]], a part of the [[Corva sector/Legends|Corva sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in the [[New Territories/Legends|New Territories]] portion of<ref name="NewTerritories" /> the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="EAOC" /> It featured the large island continent [[Klypash]].<ref name="AlienEncounters" />

The [[Shatras]], a [[Species/Legends|species]] of four-[[Eye/Legends|eyed]] [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Reptile/Legends|reptilians]], evolved on Trascor. The species was divided into five races, including the [[Hy'tras]] and the dominant [[Y'tras]], and the Shatras fought extensive racial wars<ref name="AlienEncounters" /> [[Standard century/Legends|centuries]] prior to<ref name="AIR">''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]''</ref> [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]].<ref name="Date">''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]'' represents a collection of [[in-universe]] reports from "one [[Standard year/Legends|year]] before the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]]," which corresponds to [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]], according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> After the conflicts, the Hy'tras willingly reverted to a more primitive [[Technology level|level]] of technology and made a bargain with the Y'tras in which the former surrendered all of their accumulated wealth in return for the latter leaving them alone.<ref name="AlienEncounters" />

Subsequently, the Hy'tras only resided on Klypash—with the other three Shatras races inhabiting other regions of Trascor—while members of the dominant Y'tras eventually left their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] for the wider [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]].<ref name="AlienEncounters" /> The Shatras [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Ssach'thirix]] hailed from Trascor.<ref name="AIR" />

==Behind the scenes==
Trascor was first mentioned in ''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]'', a [[1995]] sourcebook for [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="AIR" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Trascor [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the planet itself, in grid square P-3.<ref name="TEA" />
